Today I woke up and looked out the window at the morning sky.
When I think about the week ahead, here is what comes to mind.
I just got off the phone with an old friend I hadn't spoken to in years.
The coffee shop on the corner was busy this morning when I walked in.
My boss called me into the office to talk about my performance.
I opened my front door to find a package waiting on the step.
The weather forecast for the weekend just came in.
I sat down at the dinner table with my family tonight.
Walking home through the park, I noticed the leaves on the trees.
I checked my bank account balance at the end of the month.
A coworker stopped by my desk to share some news.
I finished the last page of the book I had been reading.
The alarm went off and I started getting ready for the day.
I looked at the photos from the trip we took last summer.
My neighbor waved at me as I was leaving the house.
The results of the project I worked on for months finally arrived.
I stood in line at the grocery store waiting to pay.
Someone asked me how I have been doing lately.
I watched the rain fall against the window all afternoon.
The kids ran into the room to tell me about their day.
I sat in traffic on the way to an appointment.
My favorite song came on the radio while I was driving.
I opened my email inbox to dozens of new messages.
The doctor came back into the room with my test results.
I spent the evening cooking a meal in the kitchen.
A stranger struck up a conversation with me on the bus.
I reflected on everything that has happened this year.
The party was just getting started when I arrived.
I looked in the mirror before heading out the door.
My phone buzzed with a message from someone I care about.
I sat by the window watching the sun go down.
The meeting wrapped up and everyone started packing their things.
I thought about the future and where my life is heading.
We gathered together to celebrate the occasion.
I took a deep breath before walking into the room.
The first thing I did this morning was make my bed.
I ran into someone from my old school at the station.
The teacher handed back the assignments to the class.
I pulled into the driveway after a long day at work.
My calendar reminded me of an appointment later today.
I unpacked my bags after getting back from the trip.
The waiter brought the food to our table.
I scrolled through the news on my phone over breakfast.
A letter arrived in the mail with my name on it.
I stepped outside to feel the air on my face.
The team got together to review how the year had gone.
I sat at my desk staring at the screen in front of me.
We drove out to the coast for the weekend.
I heard the front door open as someone came home.
The lights dimmed as the show was about to begin.
I wrote down a list of things I wanted to do.
My friend asked if I wanted to grab lunch together.
I watered the plants on the balcony this morning.
The bus pulled up right as I reached the stop.
I opened the curtains and let the light into the room.
We sat around the fire talking late into the night.
I got an unexpected call from a number I didn't recognize.
The line at the bank moved slowly this afternoon.
I packed my lunch and headed out the door.
Someone left a note on my desk while I was away.
I looked at my reflection in the train window.
The smell of dinner filled the whole apartment.
I met up with the group at the usual spot.
My manager sent a message about the upcoming changes.
I walked along the river on my way back home.
The phone rang just as I was sitting down to eat.
I flipped through old letters I had saved in a drawer.
We spent the afternoon walking around the city.
I turned the key and the car started up.
The store was decorated for the season when I went in.
I sat on the porch with a cup of tea.
My sister called to tell me about her weekend.
I cleared off my desk at the end of the day.
The road stretched out ahead of me on the drive.
I checked the time and realized how late it was.
We raised our glasses to make a toast.
I stepped onto the train as the doors were closing.
The morning light came through the kitchen window.
I found an old photograph tucked inside a book.
My colleague asked how the project was going.
I locked up the house before leaving for the trip.
The crowd filled the square for the event.
I sat quietly and thought about the day I'd had.
We shared stories around the dinner table.
I opened the box to see what was inside.
The kettle whistled as I waited in the kitchen.
I crossed the street toward the building ahead.
My friend showed up at the door unannounced.
I read the message twice before putting the phone down.
The garden looked different now that spring had come.
I climbed the stairs to my apartment after work.
We talked for hours about everything and nothing.
I set the table as the guests began to arrive.
The plane touched down and we taxied to the gate.
I looked back on the choices that brought me here.
My day started the moment my feet hit the floor.
We watched the fireworks light up the sky.
I closed my laptop and leaned back in the chair.
The room went quiet as everyone turned to listen.
I stepped out into the street and started walking.
